# pdf-mcp-server

> First-class CLI and MCP (Model Context Protocol) server for everything PDF:
> form filling, page operations, OCR, metadata, signatures, redaction, image
> and table extraction, batch processing, and LLM-backed document understanding.

## Overview

- Name: pdf-mcp-server
- Purpose: 57-tool PDF processing suite exposed as both a verb-first CLI (`pdf-mcp`) and an MCP server for Cursor/Claude Desktop; covers forms, pages, text, extraction, metadata, signatures, OCR, AI/VLM analysis, batch ops, and security.
- Language(s): Python
- License: Apache-2.0 (pymupdf dependency is AGPL-3.0)
- Authoritative README: <https://github.com/nfsarch33/pdf-mcp-server#readme>
- Source of truth branch: `main`

## Quick Start

- Install:
  ```
  git clone https://github.com/nfsarch33/pdf-mcp-server.git
  cd pdf-mcp-server
  make install
  ```
- CLI usage:
  ```
  pdf-mcp --help
  pdf-mcp form get-pdf-form-fields --json '{"pdf_path":"sample.pdf"}' --pretty
  pdf-mcp extract extract-text --json '{"pdf_path":"doc.pdf","engine":"auto"}' --pretty
  ```
- Run as MCP server: `pdf-mcp serve`
- Run tests: `make test`
- Minimum runtime prerequisites: Python 3.10+, uv

## Key Concepts

- 57 tools across 10 verb groups: form (9), pages (8), text (13), extract (7), metadata (4), sign (6), ocr (2), ai (4), batch (2), security (2)
- CLI and MCP share the same `pdf_mcp.registry` source of truth
- LLM backend priority: local VLM (free) > Ollama (free) > OpenAI (paid)
- Privacy-first: local processing by default; remote LLM requires explicit opt-in
- Full per-tool reference: `USAGE.md`
- Changelog: `CHANGELOG.md`
- Testing: 526 tests, 75% line coverage gate in CI

## Runnable Examples

- Read form fields: `pdf-mcp form get-pdf-form-fields --json '{"pdf_path":"sample.pdf"}' --pretty`
- Fill a form: `pdf-mcp form fill-pdf-form --json '{"input_path":"in.pdf","output_path":"out.pdf","data":{"Name":"Jane"}}'`
- OCR a scan: `pdf-mcp extract extract-text --json '{"pdf_path":"scanned.pdf","engine":"ocr"}'`
- Check LLM backends: `pdf-mcp ai get-llm-backend-info --pretty`

## Related Repos

- [cursor-tools](https://github.com/nfsarch33/cursor-tools) — Cursor IDE hook engine
